prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Indices for Formaldehyde**

Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were computed using a single-point energy calculation with Density Functional Theory (DFT), employing the **B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set** (corresponding to the `standard` tier in PySCF). The calculation was performed in the **gas phase** with a **charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1**. The input geometry was provided by the user from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz`; the geometry was not optimized. The calculation used the PySCF software. The Self-Consistent Field (SCF) converged, and no warnings were reported.

The calculated Fukui indices for each atom are as follows (values are dimensionless):

| Atom | Symbol | f+ (Electrophilic) | f- (Nucleophilic) | f0 (Radical) | Dual Descriptor (Δf) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | O | 0.46814 | 0.46742 | 0.46778 | 0.00071 |
| 2 | C | 0.70839 | 0.11244 | 0.41042 | 0.59595 |
| 3 | H | -0.08827 | 0.21008 | 0.06090 | -0.29835 |
| 4 | H | -0.08826 | 0.21006 | 0.06090 | -0.29832 |

The **most electrophilic site** is the Carbon atom (Atom 2) with an f+ index of **0.70839**.
The **most nucleophilic site** is the Oxygen atom (Atom 1) with an f- index of **0.46742**.

The integrity of the calculation is trustworthy.
